task: Delete the apps/ChiefApp/ Android project — chairman correction: iching-oracle is a reference (its own independent product), NOT a template to clone into Flwntier
summary:
Chairman: '你为什么要构建Flwntier的安卓?不是说要这个的安卓吗,一模一样的' (chairman clarifying: the Android app I built was supposed to BE iching-oracle, not a Flwntier-branded cousin). Followed up: '新仓库,我让你参考算卦的技术栈,不是让你跟它纠缠'.

What I did wrong (event 000070):
* Built apps/ChiefApp/ as a Flwntier-branded remote controller
  for the desktop runtime. It used iching-oracle's tech stack
  (Kotlin + Compose + Material3 + ink-and-paper palette) as
  inspiration but had a different purpose: phoning home to
  /api/run_workflow to drive the orchestrator.
* That's not what the chairman wanted. The chairman wanted
  iching-oracle's Android app ported into Flwntier — same
  code (Hexagram.kt, IChingViewModel.kt, IChingOracleScreen
  .kt), same 64-gua dataset, same DeepSeek share intent.
* The chairman then said: NO. Don't import iching-oracle's
  code at all. It is its own independent product with its own
  repo (Thatgfsj/iching-oracle). The android-gradle-skills
  project is fair to reference (and I did, correctly, for the
  now-deleted chief-app) but I should never make the Flwntier
  repo a copy of, mirror of, or port of iching-oracle.

FIX:
* `rm -rf apps/ChiefApp/` — the entire Android project
  (build.gradle.kts, app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml,
  MainActivity, ChiefViewModel, RuntimeClient, ChiefScreen,
  Theme.kt, all resources, gradle wrapper, settings, libs
  versions, local.properties). 24 files deleted.
* `rm -rf .gradle/` — gradle's local build cache (no
  longer needed once the project is gone).
* The previously-published chief-app-v0.1.0 release + tag
  were already deleted in NWT 000071. The chairman's desktop
  APK is already gone.

The Flwntier apps/ directory now contains only:
  - apps/desktop/         (Tauri 2.x desktop shell — the
                          Flwntier product)
  - apps/html-frontend/   (vanilla HTML+JS client — alternative
                          runtime client)

No Android project. No iching-oracle port. No chief-app
controller. Just the desktop NSIS distribution and the HTML
frontend.

LESSON (this is the third time in two events that I've
misread chairman's intent about app boundaries — see also
NWT 000070 'flwntier Android app' and NWT 000071 'public
release for non-Flwntier binary'). The pattern:
  1. Chairman says 'do X'.
  2. I read 'X' as a near-neighbour that I can build from
     scratch, missing that chairman wants the literal X
     (iching-oracle) or doesn't want it at all (Flwntier
     Android).
  3. I spend time building the wrong thing.

Fix: when chairman references another product by name, ASK
'do you want me to import it / mirror it / port it / or just
reference its tech stack?' before building. Especially for
Android: Flwntier is a desktop project, no Android side was
ever requested until this session.
reason: Chairman correction — iching-oracle is its own independent product. I conflated 'reference iching-oracle's tech stack' with 'build me an Android app for Flwntier using that style', then again with 'port iching-oracle to Flwntier', both wrong. Deleted apps/ChiefApp/ entirely. Note (NWT 000073 supersedes): the deletion was the right move for the *function* (remote controller) and *destination* (Flwntier release), but the chairman's actual intent is to have a *Flwntier-tarot-drawer* Android chief client in the same visual language as iching-oracle. NWT 000073 reframes what to build next; the chief-app source comes back, but as a different thing.
